TABLE III RESULTS OF CONFIXAGENT IN FIXING DEADLOCK, WHERE d1, d2, AND diningPhilosophers BELONG TO RESOURCE DEADLOCKS, WHILE THE REMAINING PROGRAMS BELONG TO COMMUNICATION DEADLOCKS.

TABLE II RESULTS OF COMPARING CONFIXAGENT WITH PFIX AND HIPPODROME ON A SET OF NON-DEADLOCK CONCURRENCY BUGS. HERE, LOCK DENOTES THE NUMBER OF LOCKS INTRODUCED IN CORRECTLY REPAIRED INSTANCES. TIME IN CONFIXAGENT REPRESENTS THE TOTAL TIME FOR CONFIXAGENT EXECUTION, EXCLUDING THE TIME SPENT ON API CALLS TO THE LLM.

TABLE VI THE IMPACT OF DIFFERENT STRATEGIES FOR UTILIZING LLMS ON REPAIR RESULTS IS SHOWN.

Fig. 2. Overview of ConFixAgent: The input is a program containing bugs, and the output is the repaired program. The iteration stops when no concurrency bugs can be detected or when the maximum number of iterations is reached.
